FAQ.TITLE: Frequently Asked Questions
FAQ.Q1: What is the UAE Corporate Tax rate?
FAQ.A1: The standard corporate tax rate is 9% on taxable income exceeding AED 375,000. Under this threshold, the rate is 0%.
FAQ.Q2: How often is my bookkeeping updated?
FAQ.A2: We reconcile transactions daily using our AI engine, giving you a real-time view of cash, profit, and tax liability.
FAQ.Q3: Do I get a dedicated accountant?
FAQ.A3: Yes. Every client is assigned a dedicated, Chartered Accountant (Big 4–trained) who reviews and signs off on all reports.
FAQ.Q4: Can you help with FTA audits?
FAQ.A4: Absolutely. We provide full representation and audit defense support in case of FTA inquiries or audits.

HOME.STAKES_C1_TITLE: FTA Penalties That Cost More Than Prevention
HOME.STAKES_C1_DESC: Late VAT filings carry FTA penalties from AED 1,000 per period, rising to AED 2,000 for repeat violations. One missed deadline costs more than a full year of professional accounting fees — and leaves a compliance record that follows your business.
HOME.STAKES_C2_TITLE: Lost Funding at the Final Stage
HOME.STAKES_C2_DESC: Banks and investors require clean, IFRS-compliant financials before approving loans or equity rounds. Disorganised books don't just delay opportunities — they destroy them at the final stage, when it matters most.
HOME.STAKES_C3_TITLE: Cash Flow Crisis — Invisible Until Too Late
HOME.STAKES_C3_DESC: Without real-time cash flow management and financial forecasting, profitable businesses run out of money. Not because the business failed — but because nobody was monitoring the numbers with precision and alerting you before the problem compounded.

HOME.LEAN_DEMO_Q1: My mainland company generated AED 450,000 revenue. Do I need to register for Corporate Tax?
HOME.LEAN_DEMO_A1: Yes. All UAE mainland companies must register for Corporate Tax, regardless of revenue size, to avoid the AED 10,000 late registration penalty. However, since your revenue is under AED 3,000,000, you are eligible to elect for Small Business Relief (SBR), meaning your tax payable will be AED 0. I can prepare your registration now.
HOME.LEAN_DEMO_Q2: What's my estimated VAT liability for Q3 based on the invoices I uploaded today?
HOME.LEAN_DEMO_A2: Based on the 42 invoices and 18 expense receipts processed today, your estimated VAT payable for Q3 is AED 12,450. Your filing deadline is Oct 28. Should I draft the FTA return?

HOME.FAQ_TALK_EXPERT_DESC: Book a free 30-minute call with one of our Chartered Accountants. No sales pitch — just honest advice for your business.
HOME.FAQ_Q1: What are UAE FTA penalties for late VAT or Corporate Tax filing?
HOME.FAQ_A1: UAE FTA late VAT filing penalties start at AED 1,000 for the first offence, rising to AED 2,000 for repeat violations, plus 2%–300% surcharges on unpaid VAT. Late Corporate Tax filing attracts AED 10,000+ fixed penalties. BookLean's proactive compliance system tracks all FTA deadlines, files on time every time, and assists with penalty waiver applications if needed.
HOME.FAQ_Q2: How long does UAE company setup take? Mainland vs Freezone — which is right?
HOME.FAQ_A2: UAE company setup takes 1–3 weeks with BookLean. Mainland (DED-licensed) companies allow unrestricted UAE trading. Freezone companies (IFZA, DMCC, Meydan, DIFC) offer 100% foreign ownership, zero import/export duty, and potential 0% Corporate Tax (QFZP). Choose Mainland for local B2B/B2C trade; Freezone for export-focused or international businesses. BookLean advises on structure and handles all documentation and post-setup compliance.
HOME.FAQ_Q3: Is my financial data safe with BookLean? Are you UAE PDPL compliant?
HOME.FAQ_A3: Absolutely. BookLean is fully UAE PDPL (Personal Data Protection Law) compliant. All financial data is encrypted with AES-256 and processed exclusively on UAE-based Azure and AWS servers — your data never leaves the UAE and is never shared with any third party. Our Hey Lean AI platform is supervised by licensed Chartered Accountants, and all systems undergo regular security audits.
HOME.FAQ_Q4: What is ESR filing in UAE and who needs to file?
HOME.FAQ_A4: UAE Economic Substance Regulations (ESR) require businesses in Relevant Activities — banking, insurance, investment fund management, holding company, intellectual property, lease-finance, shipping, and distribution — to demonstrate genuine UAE economic substance annually. Penalties for non-compliance start at AED 10,000–50,000. BookLean provides full ESR assessment, annual notification filing, substance documentation, and FTA audit support.
HOME.FAQ_Q5: Does BookLean provide Virtual CFO services in UAE? What does a vCFO do?
HOME.FAQ_A5: Yes. BookLean's Virtual CFO service gives UAE businesses strategic financial leadership at a fraction of a full-time CFO's cost (typically AED 30,000–60,000/month). Our vCFO service includes monthly management accounts and MIS reporting, cash flow forecasting, budget vs actual analysis, board-level presentations, investor-ready financial modelling, fundraising support, and strategic tax planning — all delivered by a dedicated Big 4-trained Chartered Accountant.
HOME.FAQ_Q6: How does UAE WPS payroll work and what does BookLean handle?
HOME.FAQ_A6: UAE Wages Protection System (WPS) is a mandatory electronic salary transfer system enforced by MOHRE. All UAE private sector employers must process salaries through WPS-approved channels. BookLean manages complete WPS payroll: salary calculation, SIF file generation, bank submission, end-of-service gratuity, leave salary accruals, MOHRE compliance reporting, and payslip distribution — handled entirely on your behalf every month.

FAQ_SUBTITLE: No jargon. Just simple, direct answers to what UAE founders ask us most — from FTA concerns to free zone rules.
FAQ_TALK_EXPERT: Talk to a Real Expert — Free
FAQ_TALK_EXPERT_DESC: Book a free 30-minute call with one of our Chartered Accountants. No sales pitch — just honest advice for your business.
FAQ_Q1: Are you licensed to handle UAE Corporate Tax?
FAQ_A1: Yes. Booklean's team includes Chartered Accountants (ICAI, ACCA, CPA qualified) with direct experience in UAE Corporate Tax (9%), VAT, and FTA compliance. We handle Corporate Tax registration, structuring, filing, and audit representation. Our AI platform is trained specifically on UAE VAT and CT law and is supervised by qualified tax experts — not generic tools.
FAQ_Q2: How is this different from just using accounting software?
FAQ_A2: Software processes transactions — it doesn't think, advise, or take responsibility. Booklean gives you the technology (AI bookkeeping, dashboards, automated reconciliation) plus a team of Chartered Accountants who review every report, handle your FTA filings, and call you when something looks off. Think of us as your full finance department — not a tool you manage.
FAQ_Q3: Can you work alongside my existing free zone auditor?
FAQ_A3: Absolutely. Many of our clients have a free zone registered auditor for statutory requirements, and we handle their day-to-day bookkeeping, VAT, Corporate Tax, and CFO advisory. We deliver clean, audit-ready books that make your annual audit faster and cheaper. We can also take over the full statutory audit if you'd like to consolidate.
FAQ_Q4: How long before my books are fully cleaned up?
FAQ_A4: For most businesses, the initial cleanup and system setup takes 2–4 weeks depending on how much history needs to be corrected. After that, your books stay clean automatically — no backlogs, no catching up. We'll give you a specific timeline after your free discovery call once we've assessed your current situation.
FAQ_Q5: Do you handle 0% Corporate Tax for Qualifying Free Zone companies?
FAQ_A5: Yes. Free Zone companies may qualify for 0% Corporate Tax as a Qualifying Free Zone Person (QFZP) under UAE CT Law Article 18. We assess your eligibility, ensure substance requirements are met, and handle registration and filing. If you don't qualify for 0%, we optimise your position under the 9% rate. We also handle mixed-activity entities where some income qualifies and some doesn't.
FAQ_Q6: Is my financial data safe with Booklean?
FAQ_A6: Completely. All data is stored in UAE-based servers (Azure/AWS UAE), encrypted with AES-256, and fully compliant with UAE PDPL regulations. We operate a zero-knowledge architecture — your data is yours. We never share, sell, or retain it beyond our service agreement.
